name: Villamartin de Campos from Palencia – circular via Villaumbrales and río Carrión
name_original: Villamartin de Campos desde Palencia – circular por Villaumbrales y río Carrión
region_intro: Road cycling around Campos, Mallorca, offers diverse landscapes ranging from flat coastal plains to gently rolling rural areas. The region is characterized by cultivated fields, traditional windmills, and proximity to the coast, providing varied scenery for cyclists. While predominantly flat, some routes incorporate slight elevation changes, particularly when extending towards other parts of Mallorca. This makes it suitable for a range of road cycling experiences, from leisurely rides to more extended excursions. Best road cycling routes around Campos The most popular road cycling route is Palencia and Monzón de Campos Castle Loop, a 34.4 miles (55.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 26 minutes to complete, showcasing historical landmarks and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Villamartin de Campos from Palencia – circular via Villaumbrales and río Carrión, an easy 23.1 miles (37.1 km) path that offers gentle gradients along the river Carrión. Local road cyclists also love the Fromista from Palencia – circular by Castillo de Sarmiento and Astudillo, a 67.0 miles (107.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Campos is defined by flat terrains, rural splendor with cultivated fields, and historical windmills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with varying elevation gains. The routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Campos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Campos?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Campos, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists of all levels. The komoot community has explored these routes extensively, with over 800 road cyclists using komoot to discover the area.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Campos?
A: Yes, Campos offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a leisurely ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158210195/villamartin-de-campos-from-palencia-circular-via-villaumbrales-and-rio-carrion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamartin de Campos from Palencia – circular via Villaumbrales and río Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 37.1 km path featuring gentle gradients along the river Carrión. Another accessible route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1160085690/palencia-and-the-castles-of-tierra-de-campos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia and the Castles of Tierra de Campos Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 46 km long and also rated as easy.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Campos?
A: The region around Campos is characterized by predominantly flat terrains and gently rolling rural areas, making it ideal for road cycling. You'll encounter cultivated fields, traditional windmills, and scenic coastal stretches. While most routes are flat, some may include slight elevation changes, especially when extending towards other parts of Mallorca, offering a mix of experiences.

Q: Are there longer routes for experienced road cyclists in Campos?
A: Absolutely. For more experienced cyclists looking for longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158326535/fromista-from-palencia-circular-by-castillo-de-sarmiento-and-astudillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fromista from Palencia – circular by Castillo de Sarmiento and Astudillo&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 107.7 km trail leading through rural landscapes and historical sites. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169312133/paredes-de-nava-from-villada-circular-via-the-puente-de-san-juan-de-becerril" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paredes de Nava from Villada – circular via the Puente de San Juan de Becerril&lt;/a&gt;, covering nearly 90 km.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Campos offers numerous scenic points. You can cycle through areas with restored historic windmills, adding a unique charm to your ride. The region also boasts beautiful rural splendor with cultivated fields and traditional stone buildings. For historical interest, consider routes that pass by sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarmiento Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/148691" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/a&gt; also offers picturesque sections, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1012824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quadruple Lock&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Campos?
A: The mild climate during autumn, winter, and spring makes Campos a favorable location for cycling throughout a significant part of the year. These seasons offer comfortable temperatures for longer rides, allowing you to fully enjoy the diverse landscapes without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Campos?
A: Yes, many routes in Campos are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158278596/palencia-and-monzon-de-campos-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia and Monzón de Campos Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1159984642/osorno-la-mayor-from-carrion-de-los-condes-circular-by-fromista-and-the-rivers-valdivia-and-carrion" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osorno la Mayor from Carrión de los Condes – circular by Fromista and the rivers Valdivia and Carrión&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243235769/osorno-valdavia-and-boedo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Osorno, Valdavia, and Boedo Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Campos?
A: The road cycling routes in Campos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from flat coastal plains to gently rolling rural areas, and the opportunity to explore historical landmarks and charming villages. The well-developed network of cycle paths, particularly around nearby Llucmajor, is also a significant draw.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by historical castles or settlements?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical sites. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158326535/fromista-from-palencia-circular-by-castillo-de-sarmiento-and-astudillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fromista from Palencia – circular by Castillo de Sarmiento and Astudillo&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sarmiento Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6599179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Astudillo&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158278596/palencia-and-monzon-de-campos-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palencia and Monzón de Campos Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; also features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6142054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5599889" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota of Astudillo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5816412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Ampudia&lt;/a&gt; on other routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Campos?
A: Campos offers routes suitable for families, especially those with flat terrains and dedicated cycle paths. The 'Cycle touring route of the windmills and beaches of Campos' is particularly easy for cycling tourism and showcases restored windmills. Routes leading to emblematic beaches like Sa Ràpita, Ses Covetes, and Es Trenc often utilize dedicated cycle lanes, providing safe and enjoyable rides for all ages.

Q: Are there routes that include stops for refreshments or cafes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for each route, the region's well-developed infrastructure and charming towns mean you'll often find services like cafes and bars along or near popular cycling paths. The old town of Campos itself retains much of its medieval charm, offering places to stop and refresh.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on road cycling routes in Campos?
A: Most road cycling routes in Campos feature relatively low elevation gains, consistent with the region's predominantly flat to gently rolling terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1243666014/we-leave-from-villada-circular-by-the-rio-sequillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;We leave from Villada — circular by the Río Sequillo&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of about 171 meters, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1166554837/villamartin-de-campos-from-ampudia-circular-by-the-laguna-de-la-nava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villamartin de Campos from Ampudia – circular by the Laguna de la Nava&lt;/a&gt; has around 246 meters. Even longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158326535/fromista-from-palencia-circular-by-castillo-de-sarmiento-and-astudillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fromista from Palencia – circular by Castillo de Sarmiento and Astudillo&lt;/a&gt;, at over 100 km, only have about 530 meters of elevation gain.
tips: Bridge and dam over the Castilla water channel, next to the ruins of an old paper mill.

Villamartín de Campos is a small town in the province of Palencia. If you're cycling through town, you can visit the 15th-century St. Salvator's Church, or venture further down to see the round brick and mud building called the Palomars, which used to house pigeons.

The Cathedral of Palencia is dedicated to San Antolín, patron saint of the city of Palencia. Popularly known as 'the unknown beauty', it was declared a Historic-Artistic Monument in 1929. The current Palencia Cathedral, in Gothic style, stands on a previous Romanesque construction, of which only a few remains remain.
